dublinCore.dc:title: 119 HR 2270 RH: Empowering Employer Child and Elder Care Solutions Act
dublinCore.dc:rights: Pursuant to Title 17 Section 105 of the United States Code, this file is not subject to copyright protection and is in the public domain.

action.action-desc: Mr. Messmer (for himself, Mr. Moolenaar, Mrs. Hinson, and Mr. Harder of California) introduced the following bill; which was referred to the Committee on Education and Workforce
Additional sponsors: Mr. Thompson of Pennsylvania, Ms. Stefanik, Mr. Fine, Mr. Harris of North Carolina, and Mr. Onder
Reported with an amendment, committed to the Committee of the Whole House on the State of the Union, and ordered to be printed
action.action-instruction: Strike out all after the enacting clause and insert the part printed in italic
For text of introduced bill, see copy of bill as introduced on March 21, 2025

form.official-title: To amend the Fair Labor Standards Act of 1938 to exclude child and dependent care services and payments from the rate used to compute overtime compensation.

section.text: This Act may be cited as the Empowering Employer Child and Elder Care Solutions Act.

section.header: Exclusion of child and dependent care in computing overtime compensation

subsection.text: Section 7(e) of the Fair Labor Standards Act of 1938 (29 U.S.C. 207(e)) is amended—

paragraph.text: in paragraph (2), by inserting payments or reimbursements for child or dependent care services; after by the employer;;
in paragraph (7), by striking or at the end;
in paragraph (8)(D)(ii), by striking the period at the end and inserting ; or; and
by adding at the end the following:
the value of any child or dependent care services provided by an employer.

subsection.text: The amendment made by subsection (a) shall apply with respect to overtime compensation required to be paid for workweeks beginning on or after the date of enactment of this Act.

endorsement.action-desc: Reported with an amendment, committed to the Committee of the Whole House on the State of the Union, and ordered to be printed
